summary: | | Classroom corps More than 130 have inquired about Troops to Teachers Troops to Teachers By Katie Pesznecker Anchorage Daily News (Published: September 28, 2002) Some balk at the idea of a soldier running a classroom. Stereotypical visions of a camouflage-clad figure barking orders collide with images of cuddly kindergartners finger-painting on colorful paper. But soldiers don't just march and shoot, military and education officials say. Military folks are well-traveled. They are used to structure, holding the attention of large groups and motivating people. They push for success. These things, officials say, make for ideal teachers. The Seafood Producers Cooperative planned to mount the fish in its office. But someone crept into a freezer, hauled off the fish and turned it into fillets. The Associated Press (Published: March 29, 2002) Juneau -- The weather in Southeast Alaska could be warmer and drier this summer because of the development of El Nino a hemisphere away. Chris Maier, a meteorologist for the National Weather Service office in Juneau, recently completed an analysis of El Nino climate data for the past 50 years.
